description: I first scanned the reservoir with my scope from a distance, looking for shorebirds. Along the northern edge of the reservoir I noticed a coot-like bird with a conspicuous red shield on its face. I walked to the dam that lines the southern edge of the reservoir and  looked at the bird again through both my scope and my binoculars. It had a dark/black body, brownish back, white stripe along the side, red shield, red beak with yellow tip, yellow-green legs. It did not vocalize. Most of the times I saw it, it was foraging along the edge of the reservoir and wending in and out of the cattails that grow along the northern edge. A few times, it swam short distances. Once I saw it walk onto a log. Of note, the bird continued to be seen by multiple observers after my observation dates. It was still present at the same location as of 4/24/2022.
